sentLog.body: Hey, saw your ad. Not sure if I'm doing this right but figured I'd give it a shot. Looking for something real, not just swiping forever. That something you can help with?
Okay cool. That's way less intimidating haha. So what do you want to know?
Hey! So I'm 40, been in St. Louis for a decade, and honestly kinda tired of the dating app thing. Is this how it works? Just text you?

Hey! Just saw your ad and figured I'd give it a shot. I'm Kevin, 28, from St. Charles. Work in logistics for a regional company here, but honestly my weekends are all about cycling the Katy Trail and taking photos—there's something about the light out there that never gets old. Looking for something real with someone who wants the same. Pretty into board games, camping, Blues hockey. Open to chatting and seeing where things go if you've got someone you think might click. What's the deal with how this works?

Honestly, that's a good question. Last serious thing was with someone really solid—she had her life together, you know? Stable job, had actual plans for the future, not just drifting. And she was genuinely kind, like she cared about people beyond just talking about it. We both valued the same things—faith mattered to us, we wanted the same direction eventually. Kids, stability, that whole picture.

But it just... fizzled. Nobody's fault really. Sometimes you want it to work but the timing's off or something's just not quite there. No drama, we're cool, just didn't click the way it needed to.

I guess what I'm saying is I'm not looking for perfect. I'm looking for someone who knows what she wants and isn't afraid to actually build something. Someone I can see a real future with, not just someone fun to hang out with for a few months. That matter for what you're doing?

yo so i've been seeing your ads around and figured why not you know, been doing the whole office admin thing in florissant for like over a decade now and honestly getting tired of the dating app swipe fatigue lol. i'm 34, been running through forest park basically every morning before work which has kinda become my thing, and i hit up karaoke nights pretty regularly too if i'm being real. anyway i'm looking for something real, like someone with ambition who actually wants to build something long term, ideally around my age range like 27-40 or so. kind of into art museums and antique shops on cherokee street when i get the chance, random anime binges too 😅 so like can you actually help me find someone or nah?

yo okay cool cool, i appreciate that 🙌 so like what do you need to know? i'm an open book honestly, been single for a minute and my friends keep saying i need to actually try instead of just going through the motions you know? like i know what i want in terms of someone with drive and ambition but i'm also pretty flexible if the vibe is right. what kind of questions you got for me?

yo so like it's kinda both honestly, the swiping thing is whatever i'm not even really on those apps that much anymore because it just feels hollow you know? like you match with someone, chat for a bit, grab coffee and like... there's just no spark and then what, move to the next person? but the real thing is even when i do go on actual dates i'm not like fully there mentally. like my head's already somewhere else thinking about work or what's coming up that week, and then it ends and i'm like well that was fine i guess but not really feeling a connection. my buddy told me last month like yo you gotta actually be present and care about getting to know someone instead of just checking boxes, and he's right you know? 😅 i think i've been protecting myself or something because like past relationships haven't worked out and so now i'm just doing the bare minimum instead of actually risking it again if that makes sense?

man okay so this is like four years ago, we dated for like two and a half years and it was just... different you know? like from the jump i was actually excited to see her, like genuinely looking forward to it instead of just going through the motions. we'd like hang out and time would just pass and i wouldn't even realize it, just talking about everything, she had her own thing going on with her job and ambitions and i really respected that. she was like into art too so we'd go to the saint louis art museum and just like spend hours there you know? and she called me out when i was slipping mentally, like she wouldn't let me get away with half-measures which is probably what i needed back then.

it just ended because like life stuff happened, she got offered this crazy job opportunity that meant moving out to the west coast and i wasn't ready to leave st louis at that point, we tried the long distance thing for like six months but it was hard and we both knew it wasn't gonna work. wasn't like some big dramatic breakup it was just... we both loved each other but we wanted different things in that moment and that's just how it goes sometimes i guess 😔

but yeah that's when i realized like i was capable of actually being present and caring and all that, which is why the last few years of just phoning it in has been so weird because i know i can do better than that you know?

yeah man that hits different hearing it said out loud like that, you're right though. i'm 34, been here in florissant my whole life basically, and like i said i'm looking for someone real, woman probably in that 27 to 40 range ideally? like i want someone who's got their own thing going on, not looking for someone to complete me or whatever that phrase is, but someone i can like grow with and actually build something with you know? someone who gets that ambition thing, like she doesn't gotta be a ceo or anything but i want someone who cares about improving herself and her life, has dreams and is actually working toward them. and honestly like if we can vibe on stuff like going to museums or just hanging out at home watching anime or whatever that's cool too, but mainly i just want someone who's present and real and willing to actually try. does that narrow it down for you or?

yo so like weekdays are pretty routine honestly, i wake up like 5:30am to hit forest park before work, been trying to get my 5k time down and it's actually finally happening which is dope, then i go to the office and do the admin thing you know, emails and scheduling and all that, it's solid work it's not like soul-crushing or anything but it's not like the most exciting either lol. i usually get lunch somewhere around midtown, then work til like 5 or 6 depending on what's going on, and honestly most weeknights i'm just like going home, throwing something on the stove, maybe working on anime or just chilling out.

but weekends are where it's at, like i'll still do a run on saturday morning because honestly i just like how it feels you know? clears my head and gets me ready for the day. then like saturday afternoon i might hit up cherokee street and check out the antique shops, just walking around looking for cool stuff, it's chill and there's good vibes on that street. sometimes i'll grab food with friends or hit karaoke on thursday nights which is always fun because like i actually have a decent voice and the 90s r&b covers hit different when you're in your zone 😅 and sunday i'm usually just like low-key, maybe go to the art museum if there's something new, meal prep a bit, watch some anime, just like recharge before the week starts again. nothing crazy but like it's a solid life you know? i'm not trying to be out every night but i'm also not trying to be a hermit either. does that help you get a picture or?

yo okay so sunday i'm just like unwinding, maybe cooking something actually good instead of just throwing stuff together, watching whatever anime i'm on right now, like i'm deep in demon slayer again actually, and just like mentally prepping for monday you know? nothing wild but it's necessary for me to just decompress and like be at peace before the work week hits again.

and yo okay so karaoke is like my thing honestly, like i can do a lot of stuff but like usher is where it's at for me, "nice and slow" or "you got it bad" like those are like my karaoke sweet spot because like the melody is smooth and i can actually add some runs without it sounding corny you know? but honestly i go hard on "i'll make love to you" by boyz ii men too, that one just hits different when you nail the lower register stuff and really lean into the emotion of it. my friends always hype me up when i do that one because like i actually sell it instead of just singing the words, you feel me? 😅 it's one of those things where like i'm not trying to be arrogant but i know i can sing and it's fun to like actually express that at karaoke instead of just being another dude at the bar yelling into a mic lol

yo okay so like dealbreakers... i don't really have like a huge list but yeah there's stuff. like first of all if someone's just looking for like casual dating or whatever that's not it for me, i've made that pretty clear that i want something real and long-term so if someone's not on that same page from the jump that's done you know? and like if someone's dishonest or like playing games that's a straight no, i've dealt with that before and it's exhausting, i'm looking for someone who can just be real with me and like communicate when something's wrong instead of letting it fester.

also like... if someone's completely closed off mentally or like doesn't have any ambitions or drive that would be tough for me, like i'm not saying she gotta be making six figures or whatever but i want someone who cares about growing and improving her life and herself, someone who's not just like coasting through existence you know? and i guess like if someone's super judgmental about stuff like my interests or my friends or how i spend my time that's a red flag, i need someone who gets that people can like multiple different things and that's cool.

and honestly if someone doesn't want to stay in the st louis area or like tries to pressure me to leave right away that's probably not gonna work, this is home you know? my runs are here, my people are here, my life is here. i'm not saying i'd never move but it's gotta be like the right time and the right person and mutual you know? like not something forced 😅 anything else you wanna know?

lol okay fair you got me, i do that don't i? 😂 okay so like if someone doesn't want to stay in the st louis area or like tries to pressure me to leave right away that's probably not gonna work because this is genuinely home you know? like my family's here, my routines are here, everything that grounds me is here. i spent enough time trying to follow someone else's dreams and compromising on what mattered to me, and i'm not trying to do that again. but like i'm also not saying i'm rigid about it, if the right person came along and we built something real together and then like down the line we decided together to move or whatever that's different, but it can't be like a condition from the start you know? it's gotta be mutual and it's gotta be because we both want it, not because one person's asking the other to just uproot everything they know. that's the thing about my ex situation, i wasn't ready to leave and she wasn't ready to stay, and that's just how it was. so yeah that's a hard no for me

hey! just saw your ad and honestly figured why not give it a shot lol. i'm aliyah, 24, based in south city. looking for something real with someone who actually has their life together, not just looking to kill time. is this how it works?
